questions.question: Spermatogenesis occurs in:
The secondary oocyte is released during ovulation and is arrested at:
Fertilization normally occurs in:
The hormone that maintains the corpus luteum in early pregnancy is:
Acrosome reaction in spermatozoa involves:

options.B: Release of acrosomal enzymes (hyaluronidase, acrosin) to penetrate zona pellucida

questions.question: The correct sequence of embryonic development is:
The diagram shows spermatogenesis. What is the ploidy of a primary spermatocyte?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of spermatogenesis in seminiferous tubule showing: spermatogonium (2n, mitosis) → primary spermatocyte (2n, DNA replicated, 4n DNA content) → Meiosis I → 2 secondary spermatocytes (n, haploid after meiosis I) → Meiosis II → 4 spermatids (n) → spermiogenesis → 4 spermatozoa (n). Sertoli cells supporting the process shown

options.B: Diploid (2n) – 2 sets of chromosomes but 4n DNA

questions.question: Implantation of the blastocyst occurs in the:
The zona pellucida is:

options.A: The outer layer of the uterus

questions.question: Sertoli cells in testes provide:

options.B: Nutritional and structural support to developing sperm (blood-testis barrier)

questions.question: The diagram shows oogenesis. How many functional eggs result from one primary oocyte?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of oogenesis: Primary oocyte (2n) undergoes Meiosis I (unequal cytokinesis) → Large secondary oocyte (n) + small first polar body. Secondary oocyte undergoes Meiosis II (only if fertilized) → Large ovum (n) + small second polar body. First polar body may also divide → 2 polar bodies. Total: 1 ovum + 3 polar bodies. Comparison with spermatogenesis (4 sperm) shown

questions.question: The placenta produces which hormone to prevent menstruation during early pregnancy?

options.B: hCG (human chorionic gonadotropin) → maintains corpus luteum → progesterone

questions.question: Capacitation of spermatozoa occurs in:
Which layer of the embryo forms the nervous system and skin?
The blood-testis barrier is formed by:
The diagram shows a cross-section of the testis. Which cells are found in the interstitium between seminiferous tubules?
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of testis showing: Multiple seminiferous tubules (with developing sperm at different stages – spermatogonia at periphery, primary/secondary spermatocytes, spermatids, spermatozoa toward lumen), Sertoli cells (large, irregularly shaped, extending from basement membrane to lumen), Leydig cells (large, lipid droplet-rich cells in interstitial tissue BETWEEN tubules), blood vessels in interstitium

questions.question: The inner cell mass (embryoblast) of blastocyst forms:
The trophoblast cells invade the endometrium during:
Human gestation period is approximately:

options.D: Both A and B are approximately correct

questions.question: Testosterone is converted to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) by:
The diagram shows the female reproductive system. Identify the structure where fertilization typically occurs.
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of female reproductive system showing: two ovaries (almond-shaped), fallopian tubes (uterine tubes) with fimbriae near ovaries and connecting to uterus at uterotubal junction, uterus (pear-shaped, thick muscular wall), cervix (lower narrow portion), vagina, broad ligament. Ampulla of fallopian tube labeled as site of fertilization. Endometrium and myometrium shown in uterine cross-section

questions.question: The corpus luteum degenerates if pregnancy does NOT occur due to:

options.B: Fall in LH levels (no hCG from blastocyst)

questions.question: Parturition (labour) is triggered by:

options.B: Oxytocin and prostaglandins causing uterine contractions (positive feedback)

questions.question: The notochord in embryo forms:

options.B: Nucleus pulposus of intervertebral discs in adults

questions.question: Neurulation is the formation of:

options.B: Neural tube (future brain and spinal cord) from ectoderm

questions.question: The diagram shows the structure of the blastocyst. Which part forms the placenta?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of blastocyst (day 5–6): Outer layer = trophoblast cells (will form placenta and extraembryonic membranes, later differentiates into cytotrophoblast and syncytiotrophoblast), Inner cell mass (embryoblast, will form the embryo proper), Blastocoel (fluid-filled cavity), Zona pellucida (still present at day 5, shed before implantation at day 6)

questions.question: Which of the following is an extraembryonic membrane that forms the fetal part of placenta?
Polyspermy is prevented after fertilization by:

options.B: Cortical reaction – hardening of zona pellucida (zona reaction)
options.C: Only one sperm can swim fast enough

questions.question: Colostrum secreted after birth is rich in:

options.B: IgA antibodies and proteins (passive immunity for newborn)

questions.question: The primitive streak in gastrulation marks:

options.B: The start of gastrulation and bilateral symmetry of embryo

questions.question: The diagram shows different phases of the menstrual cycle. What structural change occurs in the endometrium during the secretory (luteal) phase?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of uterine (endometrial) cycle corresponding to ovarian cycle: Menstrual phase (days 1–5, endometrium shed), Proliferative/follicular phase (days 6–13, estrogen causes endometrium to thicken), Ovulation (day 14), Secretory/luteal phase (days 15–28, progesterone causes glands to secrete glycogen, uterus prepared for implantation). Cross-sections of endometrium at each phase shown

options.C: Endometrial glands secrete glycogen (uterus prepared for implantation)

questions.question: Ectopic pregnancy occurs when:

options.A: Implantation occurs in anterior wall of uterus
options.B: Blastocyst implants outside uterus (usually fallopian tube)
options.D: Placenta is positioned low in uterus

questions.question: The umbilical cord contains:

options.B: 2 arteries and 1 vein (umbilical vein carries oxygenated blood to fetus)

questions.question: hCG (human chorionic gonadotropin) is detected in:

options.B: Urine and blood (basis of pregnancy test)

questions.question: Identical (monozygotic) twins arise from:

options.A: Two eggs fertilized by two sperm
options.B: Splitting of one zygote/blastocyst into two embryos

questions.question: Which layer of the uterus undergoes contractions during labour?
The placenta acts as a barrier against:

options.B: Most bacteria (but not viruses or some protozoa like Toxoplasma)

questions.question: The diagram shows a cross-section of a seminiferous tubule. Identify the cell that forms the blood-testis barrier.
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of seminiferous tubule showing: basement membrane at periphery, Sertoli cells (large columnar cells with irregular nuclei extending full width of tubule, tight junctions between adjacent Sertoli cells forming blood-testis barrier in middle region), spermatogonia (at periphery below barrier), primary and secondary spermatocytes and spermatids above barrier (adluminal compartment), spermatozoa in lumen

questions.question: In vitro fertilization (IVF) involves:

options.B: Fertilization of egg outside body, then transfer of embryo to uterus

questions.question: Relaxin hormone during pregnancy:

options.B: Softens ligaments of pubic symphysis to widen birth canal

questions.question: Sperm capacitation involves:

options.B: Changes to plasma membrane increasing motility and enabling fertilization

questions.question: The mesonephric (Wolffian) duct in male embryo develops into:
Placenta previa refers to:
The amniotic fluid functions to:

options.B: Cushion and protect fetus, allow movement, maintain temperature

questions.question: Which hormone causes descent of testes into scrotum before birth?
The diagram shows the stages of cleavage. What is the 8–16 cell stage called?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of early embryonic development: Zygote (1 cell, fertilized egg) → 2-cell stage (first cleavage) → 4-cell stage → 8-cell stage → Morula (16–32 solid ball of cells, 3–4 days) → Blastocyst (hollow ball with inner cell mass and fluid-filled blastocoel, day 5–6) → Implantation (day 6–7). Each stage labeled with timing

questions.question: Prolactin inhibits:

options.B: GnRH → suppresses ovulation during lactation (lactational amenorrhoea)

questions.question: The secondary sexual characters in males at puberty are due to:
ICSI (Intracytoplasmic sperm injection) is used when:

options.B: Severe male infertility (very low sperm count/motility)

questions.question: The neural plate is induced by:
